changes.motivation: Users needed explicit instructions to enable AWS IAM Identity Center before the portal URL step, since the workflow previously jumped straight to URL configuration without verifying the service was active.
changes.summary: Added a two-step structure to the 'Get Federated Auth User Configuration' task in `setupFederatedAuth.ts`: Step 1 attempts `getIdentityCenterInstanceId` and, on failure, shows a confirm prompt with a direct console link (`https://{region}.console.aws.amazon.com/singlesignon/home?region={region}`) instructing the user to enable the service. The validate callback re-runs the lookup after the user confirms, looping until it succeeds or the user refuses. Step 2 retains the original portal URL change prompt, now gated on `!ctx.awsSignInUrl` separately.

changes.motivation: The `navigatedToExternIdPPage` deep link URL required the account-specific IAM Identity Center instance ID, which varies per AWS account and could not be hardcoded.
changes.summary: Created `packages/cli/src/util/aws/clients/getSSOAdminClient.ts` (factory following the existing `createAWSClient` pattern) and `packages/cli/src/util/aws/getIdentityCenterInstanceId.ts` (calls `ListInstancesCommand`, extracts the instance ID from the ARN, throws `CLIError` if none found). The instance ID is resolved in Step 1 and reused to build the `#/instances/{id}/settings$identitySourceWizard` deep link for the External IdP page prompt.

changes.motivation: `@inquirer/confirm` has no native `validate` option, and a while-loop workaround rendered errors outside the Listr task output rather than inline under the prompt â€” inconsistent with how `@inquirer/input` and `@inquirer/password` display validation errors.
changes.summary: Created `packages/cli/src/util/context/confirmWithValidate.ts`: a custom Inquirer prompt built with `createPrompt` from `@inquirer/core`, mirroring `@inquirer/confirm` source but adding `errorMsg` state and returning `[promptLine, errorLine]` on validation failure â€” matching the exact inline error UX of `@inquirer/input`. Updated `Logger.confirm` in `logger.ts` to accept an optional `validate: (value: boolean) => boolean | string | Promise<string | boolean>` parameter; when provided, uses `confirmWithValidate` instead of the native confirm, with error-color styling applied to string return values.

changes.motivation: All confirm prompts in the SSO add flow previously threw `CLIError` when the user answered 'no', causing hard crashes with no opportunity to retry. Inline validation provides a better UX by keeping the user in the prompt loop.
changes.summary: Converted all five `confirm â†’ if (!confirmed) throw CLIError` patterns in `setupFederatedAuth.ts` to use the new `validate` option: the IAM Identity Center enable prompt, portal URL change prompt, External IdP page navigation prompt, metadata download prompt, metadata upload prompt, and sync re-run prompt all now return the error string on invalid answers (including 'no'), re-displaying the prompt inline until the user completes the step.

changes.motivation: New AWS SDK and Inquirer dependencies were needed for the instance ID lookup and custom confirm prompt.
changes.summary: Added `@aws-sdk/client-sso-admin@3.1024.0`, `@inquirer/core@10.3.2`, and `@inquirer/type@3.0.10` to `packages/cli/package.json`. Removed `@inquirer/type` from the root `package.json` where it had been incorrectly installed.

problems: `replace_all: true` on `globalRegionData.aws_region` also replaced the right-hand side of its own initializer (`const awsRegion = globalRegionData.aws_region` became `const awsRegion = awsRegion`), requiring a manual follow-up fix. The self-referential assignment caused a TypeScript error that was only caught on the next `tsc --noEmit` run.

`bun add @inquirer/type` was run from the repo root instead of `packages/cli/`, silently installing the package into the root `package.json`. The monorepo workspace structure means package installs must be run from the correct sub-package directory, but there is no guard or warning about this.

`@inquirer/confirm` silently ignores unknown config keys â€” passing a `validate` key to the native prompt caused no error or warning, making it non-obvious that validation was being silently dropped. Discovering this required reading the `@inquirer/confirm` type definitions.

The `ListrInquirerPromptAdapter.run()` return type is `Promise<Promise<T> & { cancel: () => void }>` rather than `Promise<T>`, requiring an `as unknown as Promise<boolean>` cast. This is an undocumented quirk of the Listr prompt adapter integration that had to be discovered through a TypeScript compiler error.

TypeScript loses narrowing of `globalRegionData.aws_region` (typed `string | undefined`) when it is referenced inside async `.catch()` callbacks, even after a null-check guard earlier in the same block. The existing codebase pattern of passing `globalRegionData.aws_region` directly to inline functions did not make this hazard visible until a compiler error surfaced it.
